pr.title: Refine gm_1A45 file split, and some small matches
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Refined the boundary between gm_1A45.c and gm_1A4C.c, moving the split point from 0x801A4CE0 to 0x801A50A0 and the corresponding data boundary from 0x803DA8C8 to 0x803DA920. The PR matched several small game-mode functions, most notably gm_801A4970, gm_801A3F48, gm_801A4CE0, and gm_801A4B1C, and brought gm_801A4D34 in gm_1A45.c to a high partial match. It also tightened prototypes and struct fields across gm, lb, baselib, and Dolphin OS headers to support the matches.
what_changed: Updated GALE01 split ranges so gm_1A45.c now owns .text 0x801A45E8-0x801A50A0 and .data 0x803DA888-0x803DA920, while gm_1A4C.c starts at 0x801A50A0 and data starts at 0x803DA920.
Moved gm_801A4CE0 out of gm_1A4C.c into gm_1A45.c, consistent with the refined split boundary.

Implemented gm_801A3F48(MinorScene*), which preloads scene data, selects HSD_SisLib allocation sizes based on MinorScene class_id, marks preload cache heap persistence, and calls DVD, snapshot, and un cleanup/setup helpers.
Replaced raw byte access in gm_801A4310 with the typed field gm_80479D30.nums.curr_major.
Implemented gm_801A4970, including debug pad-trigger handling that prints class/object stats, entity/thread info, leak information, and toggles a gm-created object via gm_80165388/gm_801653C8.
Implemented gm_801A4B1C as a setup wrapper for gm_801A4B08(fn_801A46F4, fn_801A47E4).

Added gm_801A4D34 to gm_1A45.c as a mostly matched main scene/game loop, handling pad queue flushing, reset switch state, frame/update loops, perf timing, draw/XFB operations, screenshot checks, and thread checks under debug level.
Added gm_803DA8C8 as a two-entry u64 mask table initialized to -1 values.

Refined gm_80479D58_t from loose placeholders into a more useful typed layout with u32 counters, u8 state bytes, bool callback function pointers, u64 masks, a void callback, an int selector, and bitfields at 0x38.
Changed gm_80479D48 from a bare HSD_GObjLibInitDataType to a static wrapper struct containing initdata plus padding, then updated accesses to gm_80479D48.initdata.
Improved prototypes for gm_80165388, gm_801653C8, gm_801677C0, lb_80019894, lbDvd_80018CF4, lbHeap_80015DF8, OSCheckActiveThreads, and related gm_1A45 functions.
Made several gm_1A3F.h declarations non-static, indicating they are externally referenced after the split/header cleanup.
smart_moves: Adjusted the split before moving code: gm_801A4CE0 was a 100% match in gm_1A4C before, but the PR intentionally moved it into gm_1A45 so file ownership matched the actual binary range.
Used typed struct fields to replace M2C_FIELD and raw pointer/byte indexing, improving readability while also supporting matching.
Kept gm_801A48A4 under #pragma dont_inline, suggesting inlining behavior mattered for preserving codegen around the matching work.
Introduced a small inline wrapper maybe_gm_801A48A4 to express conditional mask computation in gm_801A4D34 without overcomplicating the main loop.
Converted callback storage in gm_80479D58_t from UNK_T to explicit function pointer types, which clarified gm_801A4B08, gm_801A4B40, and the main loop call sites.
Updated cross-module prototypes only where new C needed them, allowing gm_1A3F and gm_1A45 implementations to compile without leaving call signatures as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Used NULL/boolean-style values instead of integer zero in newly understood pointer and bool contexts.
decomp_lessons: When a function at a file boundary matches but logically belongs to the previous translation unit, refine config/GALE01/splits.txt and move the function rather than preserving the old split for convenience.

A matched function may become a 'broken match' in the old unit when it is intentionally moved; evaluate bot reports in terms of net ownership and final unit boundaries, not only per-old-file regressions.
Scene-loop functions often require many supporting prototypes across gm, lb, baselib, GX/VI/perf, and Dolphin OS headers; matching the large loop is easier after tightening the leaf-call signatures.
Replacing M2C_FIELD with named struct members can unlock cleaner codegen and makes later work on the same global state structure more searchable.
Bitfields can be appropriate for packed state flags when the generated access pattern shows individual bits, as in gm_80479D58_t.unk_38_0.
For game-mode state globals, distinguish frame counters, draw counters, state bytes, callback pointers, and masks rather than leaving everything as UNK_T or padding.
Use domain-specific struct fields when known, such as GameState.nums.curr_major and PreloadCacheScene.is_heap_persistent, instead of equivalent casts or raw offsets.
naming_conventions: Boundary-local unknown functions continue to use address-based names such as gm_801A4970, gm_801A4D34, and fn_801A4BD0.
Static/global data keeps address-based names such as gm_80479D58, gm_80479D48, gm_804D6720, gm_804D6724, gm_804D6728, and gm_803DA8C8.
Structs for unknown globals use the address plus _t suffix, as in struct gm_80479D58_t.
Temporary field names remain offset-based unk_0, unk_4, unk_8, unk_C, unk_10, etc., but are progressively typed.
Bitfield names used an offset-plus-bit convention, e.g. unk_38_0 and unk_38_1.
Function pointer fields were still named by offset, but their types were made explicit: bool (*unk_14)(void), bool (*unk_18)(void), void (*unk_30)(void).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Refined splits.txt ranges to shift functions/data between gm_1A45.c and gm_1A4C.c, aligning source units with the binary addresses.
Applied #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around gm_801A48A4 to preserve non-inlined code generation.
Used PAD_STACK annotations in gm_801A4970 and gm_801A4D34, indicating stack padding was needed for matching.
Introduced a static wrapper struct around HSD_GObjLibInitDataType for gm_80479D48 to preserve layout/padding while allowing typed access through initdata.
Changed return types and parameter types from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete u8, u32, void, bool callbacks, u8*, and MinorScene-related pointers to improve callsite codegen.
Used a typed u64 mask table gm_803DA8C8[2] and -1ULL behavior to model control-mask operations in gm_801A4D34.
Moved gm_801A4CE0 into gm_1A45.c so its address 0x801A4CE0 lives in the same C file as the expanded split range.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 30.96%, a +0.02% change and +648 bytes.
The bot reported 4 new 100% matches: gm_801A4970 (+408 bytes), gm_801A3F48 (+204 bytes), gm_801A4CE0 (+84 bytes), and gm_801A4B1C (+36 bytes).
The bot reported gm_801A4D34 in gm_1A45 improved by +799 bytes to 91.32%.
The bot also reported gm_801A4CE0 as a broken match in gm_1A4C, which is explained by the intentional file split move rather than evidence of a lost implementation.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching gm_801A4D34 after this 91.32% partial match.
Search for subsequent renames or field refinements of struct gm_80479D58_t.
Look for later PRs that refine gm_80479D48 layout or replace its padding with a named field.
Check whether gm_804D6728 and gm_804D6724 receive concrete types beyond UNK_T and void callback.
Search for future split adjustments around gm_1A45.c and gm_1A4C.c to see if 0x801A50A0 remained stable.
